methods.description: Process for analyzing long-chain alkenones in lake sediment samples to study past climate conditions.

steps.description: Freeze-dried samples were extracted ultrasonically (3 × 30 min) with a solvent of dichloromethane: methanol (9:1, v/v, 15 ml each time).
steps.searchable_summary: extract lipids from freeze-dried samples using ultrasonic extraction with dichloromethane and methanol

steps.description: The total lipids were dried under N2 and saponified with 6% KOH in MeOH for at least 12 h at room temperature.
steps.searchable_summary: dry lipids under nitrogen and saponify with KOH in methanol

steps.description: 1.5 ml of NaCl in water (5%, w/w) was added, and the neutral solution was extracted with 4 ml n-hexane.
steps.searchable_summary: add NaCl solution and extract neutral lipids with n-hexane

steps.description: The neutral solution was then separated into three fractions with silica gel column chromatography using 4 ml n-hexane, 4 ml dichloromethane, and 4 ml methanol, respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: separate neutral lipids into fractions using silica gel column chromatography

steps.description: The alkenone fraction was analyzed on an Agilent 7890 Gas Chromatography with Flame Ionization Detection (FID) at The University of Hong Kong, using n-C36 n-alkane as an internal standard for quantification.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze alkenone fraction using gas chromatography with FID

steps.description: Each sample was injected in a splitless mode, with H2 as carrier gas. The oven temperature program for alkenone analysis was 60 °C (1 min) to 270 °C at 13 °C /min, and then to 310 °C at 3 °C /min (held 30 min). The laboratory standards were repeatedly analyzed to assess the analytical precision.
steps.searchable_summary: validate analytical precision using repeated analysis of standards
